name: Grade 1 Spirit — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: Family-directed, non-devotional pathway
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Spirit pathway.

units.title: Investigate: ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to ethical choices, service, reflection, and respectful dialogue.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
